These logical gaps are central to whether the section is 'solid.'",

Specific numeric claims (84.2, 56.1, 67.4, 47.9, 49.3 PQ) and competitor figures (InternImage-H 83.5, ViT-Adapter 55.3) require verification against source tables. The ablation claim of '1.2 mIoU average' referenced in Section 5 is asserted but not shown here. Failure to validate any of these invalidates the delegation.",

rationale.R1.2: Several internal consistency failures present: (1) the opening claim of 'consistent state-of-the-art performance across all benchmarks' is contradicted by Pascal-Context, where the method only matches (not surpasses) prior SOTA; (2) COCO-Stuff's 47.9 mIoU is reported without any baseline comparison, unlike all other benchmarks; (3) 'panoptic segmentation track of COCO-Stuff' is suspect—COCO-Stuff is a semantic segmentation dataset; COCO Panoptic is a distinct task/dataset. These logical gaps are central to whether the section is 'solid.'
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: 'solid' in a results section means the numbers, comparisons, and the causal attribution chain (gains → cross-attention module → ablation) must all hold. Specific numeric claims (84.2, 56.1, 67.4, 47.9, 49.3 PQ) and competitor figures (InternImage-H 83.5, ViT-Adapter 55.3) require verification against source tables. The ablation claim of '1.2 mIoU average' referenced in Section 5 is asserted but not shown here. Failure to validate any of these invalidates the delegation.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact makes strong novelty claims ('strongest unified segmentation model on standard benchmarks', plug-compatible module, cross-attention as the decisive innovation). These claims need delta-assessment against nearest prior work. The mechanism attribution is also partially unsupported—'selectively attends to high-confidence boundary regions and ignores ambiguous interior pixels' is an interpretive claim not grounded in the evaluation results shown.
rationale.R1.7: Competitor attribution is load-bearing for trust: InternImage-H and ViT-Adapter scores must match published numbers. The omission of any citation for the Pascal-Context and COCO-Stuff SOTA baselines, and the absence of a citation for the claimed medical imaging parallel at the close, create traceability gaps that undermine the section's credibility.
